title: Reset Password Policy and Procedure
content: Users can securely reset their TrueAILab account passwords at any time. To perform a password reset, navigate to the Settings section in the top-right corner of the user dashboard, click on 'Security', and locate the 'Password Management' block. Enter your current password followed by your newly selected password, ensuring it meets our strength standard (minimum 12 characters, including at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, and one special symbol). If you have forgotten your password and cannot log in, click the 'Forgot Password' link on the sign-in portal. A secure single-use recovery link will be sent to your registered email address, valid for precisely 15 minutes.

title: Account Deletion and Data Retention
content: To completely delete your TrueAILab account, navigate to the 'Account settings' page and scroll down to the red zone labeled 'Danger Zone'. Click on 'Terminate Account and Purge Data'. You will be prompted to re-enter your password and supply your 2FA verification code to confirm the action. Once approved, the account will be immediately deactivated. In compliance with general data protection regulations (GDPR), all associated workspace files, prompt templates, API configuration histories, and account metadata will be permanently deleted from active databases within 14 calendar days. Billing data is retained in archived ledgers for up to 7 years to meet tax obligations.

title: API Keys Management and Security Controls
content: TrueAILab provides high-throughput API endpoints for programmatic access to our model suites. Developers can generate, rotate, and revoke custom API keys by visiting the 'Developer Console' from the main dashboard navigation drawer. Each active API key has granular permissions, including read-only, write-only, or full administrative scopes. For security compliance, API keys are only displayed in full once upon initial creation. Users are strongly advised to store keys securely using dedicated environment variables or standard vaults (like Vault or AWS Secrets Manager) and never hardcode them in version-controlled systems. TrueAILab employs automated scanners that proactively search public code hubs for leaked credentials; any detected key is immediately revoked for protection.

title: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Implementation
content: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) provides an additional layer of login protection. To enable 2FA, navigate to Settings > Security and scroll to the section labeled 'Multi-Factor Settings'. Click 'Enable 2FA' and use any standard authenticator app (such as Google Authenticator, Authy, or Microsoft Authenticator) to scan the presented QR code. Enter the temporary six-digit verification code generated by the app to finalize the connection. If you lose your mobile device, you can bypass the prompt using one of the single-use backup recovery codes generated during your initial setup; these are found in Settings > Security > Backup Codes. Losing both your authenticator device and your recovery codes will require manual identity verification via support.

title: Data Portability and Workspace Export
content: TrueAILab workspace owners have the right to request comprehensive data exports for auditability or backup purposes. Workspace exports gather all active document sources, vector indexing statistics, active settings logs, custom templates, and chat transcript indices. To run an export, go to Settings > Data Management and click on 'Initiate Workspace Export'. Exports take between 5 to 30 minutes depending on files volume. Once completed, a secure download link containing a compressed zip file with structural JSON metadata and files will be made available. The download link is highly confidential and expires automatically after 24 hours.

title: Workspace Roles and Collaboration Permissions
content: Workspaces in TrueAILab support collaborative model exploration and are governed by strict user roles. The three primary roles are Owner, Admin, and Member. Owners have full ownership, billing control, and authorization to delete the entire workspace. Admins can invite new users, generate shared API keys, index new RAG documents, and adjust overall workspace configurations, but cannot delete the workspace or edit billing details. Members can execute prompts, interact with custom chat sessions, and review knowledge documents but cannot alter global configurations or upload new documents.

title: Slack Integration Setup Guide
content: You can connect your TrueAILab assistant directly to your company's Slack workspace for collaborative RAG prompting. To configure the integration, go to Settings > Integrations > Slack and click on 'Connect Workspace'. You will be redirected to Slack's authorization portal, where you must select a destination workspace and grant permissions to view messages and post replies. Once authorized, invite the TrueAILab bot to any public or private Slack channels using the command `/invite @TrueAIAssistant`. Users can trigger the grounded bot within channel chats by mentioning it directly (e.g., `@TrueAIAssistant check refund policy`).

title: Billing and Refund Policy
content: TrueAILab operates a credit-based subscription billing model, structured into Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers. Monthly billing cycles begin on the date of subscription activation. If you are unsatisfied with your subscription, you can request a full refund within a 14-day window from the initial purchase date, provided your aggregate API usage has not exceeded 1,000,000 processed tokens. To request a refund, open a support ticket under the billing category. Refund approvals are processed by our finance department within 3 to 5 business days, and funds are credited back to the original payment method.
